text.title: hybrid-delivery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for hybrid-delivery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Boundary ambiguity — hybrid is declared but the line between agile-here and predictive-there is never written down.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every governance decision becomes a negotiation over which method applies. Teams default to the method that benefits them locally, creating incoherence at the program level.

failure-mode.description: Dual definitions of done — agile arm calls a story "done" when it passes sprint review; predictive arm calls a deliverable "done" when a document is signed. No translation layer exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Steering-committee status reports are incoherent. A sprint's "80% done" and a milestone's "waiting for signature" cannot be meaningfully combined without a defined mapping.

failure-mode.description: Tool sprawl — backlog in Jira, Gantt in MS Project, deliverables in SharePoint, risks in Excel. No canonical source of truth.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each tool sync gap creates an opportunity for data to diverge. Agents and PMs spend more time reconciling tool disagreements than managing project risk.

failure-mode.description: Governance drift — predictive arm enforces a stage gate; agile arm flies through it silently. Or agile gets gated to a quarterly cadence and loses all iteration value.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gate frequency must match actual risk profile. Over-gating agile destroys delivery speed without risk benefit; under-gating predictive work accumulates unbounded risk.

failure-mode.description: Political compromise disguised as method — hybrid is adopted because PMO wants control and teams want autonomy, not because project characteristics require it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Method choice must follow project fit. When hybrid is a political settlement, neither side commits to the other's discipline, producing incoherent execution with the overhead of both methods.

failure-mode.description: EVM applied to the agile arm — SPI and CPI computed from story points multiplied by an hourly rate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Story points are a relative sizing unit, not a labour cost measure. EVM on agile produces SPI/CPI numbers that look precise but measure nothing real. Report agile metrics in agile units; apply EVM only to the predictive arm.

failure-mode.description: Agents over-summarize hybrid programs as "behind" because they conflate vocabulary; force agile metrics in agile units, predictive metrics in predictive units.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Conflation of vocabulary leads to false rollup conclusions that miss real risks in one arm while over-focusing on the other.

failure-mode.description: LLMs auto-translate epics into "deliverables" and lose information (acceptance criteria collapse to one line). Keep both views; never destroy detail.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Detail loss breaks traceability and makes it impossible to recover original requirements when the inevitably questions arise later.

failure-mode.description: Date arithmetic is unreliable across calendar conventions (work days vs. calendar days, fiscal vs. ISO weeks). Hard-code the convention in the prompt; have a tool, not the LLM, compute deltas.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM date math is a known failure mode; systematic errors in burn calculations cascade to wrong gate-readiness assessments.

failure-mode.description: Stage-gate "go / no-go" must never be auto-emitted — flag readiness only, leave the call to the sponsor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gate decisions are inherently human / sponsor decisions; auto-emission removes accountability and trains stakeholders to ignore readiness assessments.

failure-mode.description: EVM on agile arm is a trap: agents will compute SPI/CPI from story points × hourly rate. Refuse unless the conversion is explicitly accepted by finance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Story points × hourly rate is not earned value; this violates EVM principles and produces misleading financial reports.

failure-mode.description: Cross-tool sync drift: if Jira and MS Project disagree, pin the system of record per field in `program.yaml`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a pinned source-of-truth per field, agents have no deterministic way to decide which value is correct during reconciliation.

failure-mode.description: Vocabulary leakage: predictive uses "milestone, deliverable, baseline"; agile uses "increment, story, velocity". Force terminology per audience.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing vocabularies in reports confuses steering-committee members and masks real misalignment between arms.

failure-mode.description: Bulk gate-pack generation is a leak risk — packs pull from confidential decks/screenshots. Run `password-scrubber-agent` and manual review before sharing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gate packs aggregate sensitive data; automated generation without scrubbing frequently leaks credentials, financial data, or strategic information.

failure-mode.description: Long-context drift: program graphs >50 epics blow context. Page by milestone or workstream and operate on slices.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Operating on massive program graphs depletes context windows and leads to loss of fidelity in agent analysis.
